BTC Ops Hub data: 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC

Counter doctrine and intel snapshots for alliance use. Numbers are model-derived or public-dump-derived — see Method before quoting anything as fact.

COUNTER MATRIX ALLIANCE INTEL THREAT BOARD TARGETS STATIONS MARKET COMBAT LOGS PVE FLEETS SECTORS TICKER FLEET ADVISOR METHOD & CAVEATS

Each cell: minimum row-type attackers to fully wipe 10 column-type defenders (1 for Dread/Titan) at ≥90% inside the round cap. Defending needs fewer — defenders keep the field on draws. Hover to cross-hair.

dominant favorable even costly bad trade ∅ cannot pierce, ever

Enemy fields X → you bring Y

Public-dump snapshot (nexuslegacytools.com), fetched 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC. Dumps lag live play by hours — verify anything time-critical in game. Facts only: no strategy is encoded here.

Top 20 alliances by military score, growth rates over the last ~6.9 days of dump history (window shown per snapshot). Click a column header to sort. Facts only; verify anything time-critical in game.

Non-BTC players sorted by distance to the nearest BTC-member planet (Euclidean, system coordinates; every alliance member's planets count as origins — the Nearest BTC column names the closest member, so any member can read their own raid range). Chips: INACTIVE Nd = zero delta on economy and overall score for N consecutive daily snapshots; VACATION = flagged vacation mode; NO ALLIANCE = active player with no alliance tag; NEW Nd = homeworld colonized ≤14 days ago (join date proxied by homeworld colonization — the dumps carry no explicit signup date). "New players only" + "no alliance only" together = recruit-scout mode: fresh unallianced players near BTC space. The search box narrows any view by typed text — matches player name, alliance tag, any of their systems ("A3-12"), their sector (short code "A3" or full "Alpha Arm - Sector 3"), or the nearest-BTC member/system — and combines with the checkboxes. Click any column header to sort. Snapshot 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC.

Caveats: vacation players cannot be attacked (tracked for awareness). PM-reported but unverified: inactive players stop producing ~24h after being flagged — treat loot expectations accordingly. Activity is inferred from daily score snapshots (a player can be active without scoring in a given window).

Every station across all six arms. Owner column shows the holding alliance's tag (or "neutral"). Capture column counts down live from the snapshotted captureEndsAt timestamp. Building levels are packed: Dock · Shield · Turret · Storage. Basics = ore + silicates + hydrogen + alloys. Click a column header to sort. Facts only, snapshot 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC.

Trade-pair rates from hub order streams (fan-site aggregation, 2026-07-14 13:09 UTC). Every rate reads as what 1 unit of the first resource fetches — e.g. hydrogen → silicates: 1.77 sil means one hydrogen trades for 1.77 silicates. Pick a hub for local rates, volumes, and live-order counts; "All hubs" shows galaxy-wide averages.

Combat losses inferred from military-score drops in the daily score-history dump (drops ≥1k, 1-day window). A drop means ships died — but scores can't attribute attacker vs defender, and observed reports say winners often lose ~nothing, so most real battles read one-sided. PvE losses (pirates, mining raids, anomalies) are included and indistinguishable. 0 systemic wave(s) filtered out (≥15% of all players dropping at once = score recalc, not battles); 2,097 loss events across 6 polling waves. Facts only, snapshot 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC. Tracked alliances in bold.

Resident losses per system (same heuristic as the hot-systems table — right for defenders, approximate for attackers). Color and dot size both encode losses; faint dots are the colonized galaxy for orientation; blue rings are BTC member systems. Hover a hot spot for the system name.

⚠ Static community data — does not auto-update. Transcribed 2026-07-13 from the player-made "Nexus Legacy Cheat Sheet" by xClaw (contributors: Jimbobicus — tech tree, Uetri — mining calculator), shared on the official Discord. Player-tested field results, not verified formulas — if the sheet updates or the game rebalances, this page lags until re-transcribed. Techs column = the submitting player's own techs (clarified 2026-07-13; different submitters explain the varying values) — sim checks stay vanilla both sides, so they're slightly conservative vs a teched player.

Sim check: every row with a recorded enemy composition was replayed on the calibrated battle engine at vanilla tech both sides (ranges checked at both ends). ✓ = engine reproduces the sheet's result · ~ = agrees at the low end of the range but bleeds/fails at the top end · ✗ = engine disagrees · n/a = no enemy comp to sim against. Verdict across the sheet: the data holds up — 14 of 15 dead-space mining rows and all pirate-camp rows reproduce exactly.

Freighters are not recommended for Open Space — use Mining Vessels.

Recommended ratio: 3 Interceptors OR 1 Cruiser per 1 Mining Vessel / Gas Collector.

At higher numbers fewer cruisers are needed; even better when you start including EW Ships.

Sim verdict on the dead-space table: 14 of 15 simmable rows reproduce exactly at vanilla tech (held rows sim at 99–100% hold, wiped rows at 0%); the 'draw' row sims as a hold with ~21 losses. The sheet and the calibrated engine corroborate each other.

Pirate raid size appears to scale with the mining fleet — the 105-vessel run drew a 1,254-ship raid and was wiped despite a 100-cruiser escort.

Sim suggestion for the 'draw' row: the sheet's BS core is right — an equal-size cruiser wall gets wiped by this raid in-model, and adding fighter chaff makes it WORSE (the raid's 59 Cruisers rapid-fire fighters ×4). Adding ~60 Cruisers to the BS core trims expected escort losses from ~21 to ~15; otherwise fly it as-is and budget ~20 escort losses.

EW Ships appear in most successful large runs; the engine models them as plain hulls (jamming not modeled), so their real contribution is likely understated here.

Anomalies

Recommended clear fleets by encounter

Zone (inferred)Encounter observedRecommended fleetSheet claimSim checkSim suggestion
Sentinel9–10 Scouts · 3–4 Fighters1 Scoutsufficient✗ sim disagrees1 Cruiser (or 5 Intys) clears the max roll clean; a lone Scout only survives if the anomaly doesn't fight
Open6–20 Scouts · 8–19 Fighters · 0–8 Intys21–25 Interceptorsoccasional Inty loss✓ sim agrees—
Open6–20 Scouts · 8–19 Fighters · 0–8 Intys7 Cruisersno losses, slightly slower✓ sim agrees—
Dead space20–38 Fighters · 11–20 Intys · 5–12 Cruisers · 2–5 BS60 Cruisersno losses (tech/Warlord dependent)~ top-end bleeds120 Cruisers for a clean top-end roll (100 Cru ≈ 1 loss)
Dead space20–38 Fighters · 11–20 Intys · 5–12 Cruisers · 2–5 BS25 Cruisers + 25 Missile Cruisersno losses (tech/Warlord dependent)✗ sim disagrees40 Cru + 40 MC (~2 losses even at max roll)

Sim verdicts (vanilla, both ends of each range): 21–25 Intys → 100% win, 0–2 losses (matches 'occasional Inty loss' exactly). 7 Cruisers → 100% / 0 losses at both ends — also matches our own independent solver from 2026-07-03. 60 Cruisers → clean at the low end but 89% / 36 losses vs a max-roll encounter. 25 Cru + 25 MC → clean at the low end but 7% win vs max roll (missile cruisers have no scout/fighter rapid-fire). The sheet's own 'highly depends on your military techs and Warlord' hedge is consistent with the top-end bleed.

The '1 Scout' small-anomaly row is not reproducible as a combat fleet (instant loss in-engine) — it likely refers to the no-pirates sentinel anomaly case (hazard only), matching Determinor's guide.

Zone labels are OUR inference — the sheet's three column groups carry no zone names. Grounds: the middle group's encounter range matches Determinor's published Open-anomaly roll (5–20 Scouts + 2–10 Fighters, hard variants add Intys) almost exactly; battleship-bearing spawns match the dead-space raid pool seen in the mining table; the trivial left group matches sentinel-zone content.

All three rows reproduce at vanilla: 100% win with 0–2 losses even at the top end of each encounter range.

Camp types per PM (in-game ground truth, 2026-07-13): Scavenger / Raider / Marauder, matched to the sheet's small→large column groups. Community reports place Marauder camps in dead-space (red) zones.

All 150 sectors from the meta dump. Note: sector-control fields (controller alliance, controlled-since) exist in the dump but are not yet populated by the game — this board lights up automatically when sector conquest goes live. Snapshot 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC.

Market hubs

Six sentinel hubs (safe) and six dead-space pirate hubs (contested), one per arm.

Rolling change log, newest day first. Diffed each publish against the previous snapshot. Cap 200 events; older than 14 days pruned. Facts only. Diff snapshot 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC.

Enter the enemy fleet (from a spy report), then either check the fleet you already have — direct verdict with expected losses on both sides and the replacement bill for your losses — or tick the hulls you can build and search counter-compositions from scratch. When your fleet falls short, the advisor searches the smallest reinforcements that turn it into a pass: additions drawn from your buildable hulls (including hulls you don't own yet — e.g. battleships you can build but haven't), stacked on top of what you have, plus plain "more of your current mix". The battle engine runs in your browser; resource bills are exact build-screen numbers, no market conversion. Recommendations are the margined size, not the knife-edge minimum: sim counts are estimates (see Method), the default adds +25%. Vanilla tech both sides. Carrier wings (card-confirmed, applied to BOTH sides, fighters first): each carrier buffs 16 fighters (+25% ATK / 15% dodge); leftover carriers wing 4 bombers each (+30% HP) — for the enemy that's the worst-case assumption. Fleet entries persist in your browser between visits.

Enemy fleet

Your fleet — what you have right now

Your options

Where these numbers come from

Counter matrix: ~950 simulation batteries on the community battle engine (nexuslegacytools). Every stat visible on in-game ship cards matches the engine exactly; shield pools remain the model's reconstruction. Round cap, damage variance, and shield regen use the fan site's calibration fitted against 144 logged real battles (constants: 15 rounds, variance 0, regen 0.4; refreshed each publish). Fit quality is rough (mean error ~0.5) — trust composition choices over exact counts. Pure-type duels at vanilla tech; mixed fleets shift rapid-fire economics substantially. The matrix's Carrier row is bare hulls — the card-confirmed 16-fighter wing buff is a mixed-fleet composition, so it lives in the Fleet Advisor (which does model it), not in a pure-type table. Bomber ×3 vs defense buildings also not shown (the engine sims fleet-vs-fleet only).

Targets / Stations / Sectors / Ticker: joined nightly from the fan site's per-arm universe dumps + player score histories. INACTIVE = consecutive daily snapshots with zero economy and overall score delta (a player can be active without scoring); VACATION comes straight from the dump and such players cannot be attacked. The claim that inactive players stop producing ~24h after being flagged is player-reported and unverified. Station stocks, shields, and capture timers are as-of the snapshot stamp; the Ticker is a day-over-day diff of consecutive snapshots, so same-day events can land a day late. Sector-control fields exist in the dump but are not yet populated by the game.

Combat: loss events are military-score drops between consecutive history points. The history arrives in polling waves (~5/day), so a "when" is really a ~4–6 hour window and co-timing between players means nothing. No attacker/defender attribution is possible from scores; winners often lose ~nothing so battles read one-sided; PvE losses included. Waves where ≥15% of all players drop at once are treated as score recalcs and excluded. Hot systems map losses to the LOSING player's home systems — right for defenders, wrong for attackers who died elsewhere.

PvE Fleets: static hand-transcription of the community "Nexus Legacy Cheat Sheet" (xClaw + contributors, shared on the official Discord) — player-tested results, NOT auto-updating and NOT verified formulas; re-transcribe when the sheet changes. Every row carrying an enemy composition is sim-checked on the calibrated engine at vanilla tech (see the chips on that tab); the mining and pirate-camp tables reproduce almost exactly, which cross-validates both the sheet and the engine.

Intel: joined from nexuslegacytools public JSON dumps (players, alliances, per-arm universe data, 7-day histories). Score/ranking snapshots only — no private account data exists in the dumps. The station/planet feed lags live play. Tracked alliances are added as we gather intel on them.

Fleet advisor: runs the same battle engine in your browser. Check my fleet sims your entered fleet directly (600 runs); on a shortfall it binary-searches the smallest reinforcement that passes — each doctrine composition from your buildable hulls added on top of your existing fleet, plus "more of your current mix" — so the suggestion can include hulls you can build but don't own yet. Find counters binary-searches each composition from scratch. Both add your safety margin and price results with exact per-resource build bills (no market conversion) — bills load automatically from every captured cost screen, so a new screenshot upgrades the advisor with no code change. Carrier wings are card-confirmed and applied to BOTH sides, fighters first: 16 fighters per carrier (+25% ATK, dodge approximated as bonus EHP), leftover carriers wing 4 bombers each (+30% HP, applied to hull) — enemy carriers are assumed to fly buffed wings too (worst case). Fleet entries and hull ticks persist in your browser (localStorage, this device only). Same model caveats as the matrix: trust the composition choice more than the exact count; the margin exists because the counts are estimates.

Wanted for calibration: shipyard cost screens for Torpedo Frigate / Battleship / Bomber (Carrier landed 2026-07-13), and ANY battle report with 100+ ships per side. Those two things upgrade every number on this page.

Compiled by McBeer89's ops toolkit · snapshot 2026-07-14 10:18 UTC · regenerate: tools/build-ops-hub.js
